stories.title: Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching Market Report 2026: Global Analysis Projects 42.8% CAGR and $9.1 Billion Valuation by 2030
stories.simple_summary: A report predicts that AI tools for teachers will become a much bigger business, growing to $9.1 billion in value by 2030.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and education companies — nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Generative AI in Architecture Market Report 2026 Now Available — 250-Page Study Covers Design Automation, Urban Planning, and Cloud Collaboration
stories.simple_summary: A study looked at how AI can help architects design buildings and plan cities more quickly and work together online better.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to architecture firms and investors — if you're not in that business, it doesn't directly affect you.

stories.title: Asian investors eye firms benefiting from but resilient to AI
stories.simple_summary: Asian investors are looking for companies that will do well even if AI doesn't deliver everything people hope for.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to investors — it shows people are being careful about which AI companies might actually succeed.

stories.title: Datacentres are a ticking timebomb. We must make sure AI’s benefits outweigh the costs | Nicki Hutley
stories.simple_summary: A writer warns that the big computer servers that power AI use so much electricity that it could become a serious problem for the environment and costs.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters because if AI keeps growing, electricity bills could go up for everyone, and environmental damage affects us all.

stories.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
stories.simple_summary: A company called Lovable is raising money and might be worth $13.2 billion soon.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
stories.simple_summary: Google built a tool that can spot when pictures are fake AI creations, and it helped prove that a picture of Senator McConnell was not real.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters because it's getting easier to catch fake pictures and videos online, which helps protect people from being tricked.

stories.title: Introducing Claude apps gateway for AWS
stories.simple_summary: Amazon announced a new way for people to use Claude (an AI assistant) through Amazon's cloud computer services.
stories.why_it_matters: This mainly matters to tech businesses who use Amazon's services — it gives them another AI tool to choose from.

stories.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
stories.simple_summary: Elon Musk's company released a new and cheaper version of an AI assistant called Grok that works more efficiently.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and AI companies comparing different tools — it doesn't change anything for your daily life.

stories.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
stories.simple_summary: A startup thinks they can train robots to be smarter by using millions of hours of video game footage instead of teaching them in the real world.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually lead to better robots, but it's early research — nothing you need to worry about or act on now.

stories.title: Opinion | Did We Make the Wrong Bet on Big A.I.?
stories.simple_summary: Someone is questioning whether betting so much money on advanced AI was the right decision.
stories.why_it_matters: This is one person's opinion about whether AI companies will succeed as investors hope — it doesn't affect your life directly.

stories.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
stories.simple_summary: Google Photos added a new feature that can improve your videos by adding better lighting, changing backgrounds, or adding artistic effects.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google Photos, you now have new tools to make your family videos and pictures look nicer without much work.

stories.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
stories.simple_summary: A CEO believes that teaching AI using video game information might be better than teaching it from the internet, because games show how things actually move.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly tech research — nothing you need to act on, but it could eventually make AI better at understanding the physical world.
